title: Treasure Trove

{{Quote|The ''Treasure Trove'' has missed it's [sic] rendezvous mark by 36 standard hours.|Major Bren Derlin|<ref name="Freedom No More" />}}
The '''''Treasure Trove''''' was one of thirty [[Gallofree Yards, Inc./Legends|Gallofree Yards]] [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transports]] stationed at the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Amm Natejeka]], the ''Treasure Trove'' was to pick up a shipment of badly needed [[Blaster gas/Legends|blaster gas]] from the [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] [[Black market/Legends|black market]] for delivery to the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] regrouping beyond the [[Galactic Rim/Legends|edge]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the wake of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. The ''Treasure Trove'' never escaped past the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of Hoth, however, crash-landing on a giant [[Asteroid/Legends|planetoid]] within the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]], one of seventeen Rebel GR-75 medium [[Transport/Legends|transports]] destroyed during the battle.

==Description==
The [[Gallofree Yards, Inc./Legends|Gallofree Yards]]<ref name="RebelTransportDatabank" /> [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] ''Treasure Trove''<ref name="GR-75 medium transport" /> served the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]].<ref name="Freedom No More" /> Measuring ninety [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length, the transport was minimally armed with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]<ref name="RebelTransportDatabank" /> and was equipped with [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight drive]]. The ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} [[Cockpit/Legends|command pod]] contained at least one [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], while the transport had a single [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]]. Cargo bays comprised the lower portion of the ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} [[Hull/Legends|hull]].<ref name="Freedom No More" />

==History==
===Battle of Hoth===
{{Quote|This is your mission that you must complete immediately. Head to the coordinates enclosed on this datapad, under file v22358. There, you are [sic] establish contact with the black market of this mining colony, and acquire a cargo of spin-sealed tibanna gas. A landing platform is also enclosed. The second set of coordinates are for our rendezvous point, which you must make within five days.|General Carlist Rieekan's datapad message to Captain Amm Natejeka, ''Treasure Trove''|<ref name="Freedom No More" />}}
[[File:SnowspeederLaunchBay-TCGtBoH.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Treasure Trove'' was stationed at the Rebel Alliance's Echo Base.]]
The ''Treasure Trove'' was a product of Gallofree Yards,<ref name="RebelTransportDatabank" /> a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] manufacturer that went bankrupt several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Many of Gallofree's remaining transports,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|317|Gallofree Yards, Inc.}}</ref> the ''Treasure Trove'' included,<ref name="Freedom No More" /> eventually found their way into the service of the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="CSWE" /> The Rebellion acquired these<ref name="RebelTransportDatabank" /> outdated<ref name="New Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref> transports at significantly reduced prices, but their affordability translated to a good deal of constant upkeep.<ref name="RebelTransportDatabank" /> By the year [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], amidst the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Date" /> the ''Treasure Trove'' came to number among<ref name="Freedom No More" /> the Rebellion's fleet of thirty GR-75 medium transports stationed at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]],<ref name="Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] headquarters on the remote [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].<ref name="GG3">[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]]</ref> During its service with Alliance High Command, the ''Treasure Trove'' fell under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Amm Natejeka]].<ref name="Freedom No More" />

[[File:Rieekan.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Carlist Rieekan (pictured) tasked Amm Natejeka with taking the ''Treasure Trove'' to the Bespin system.]]
When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] invaded Echo Base during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the Rebel evacuation plan called for each GR-75 medium transport to attempt a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] run through the line of [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] in Hoth's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] under dual [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] escort and the protective covering fire of the base's [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|v-150 Planet Defender]] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]]. Just prior to the evacuation, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], theater [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of all Rebel forces in the [[Hoth system/Legends|Hoth system]],<ref name="GG3" /> tasked Natejeka with taking the ''Treasure Trove'' to the nearby [[Bespin system/Legends|Bespin system]] following its departure from Hoth, where she would make contact with the [[Black market/Legends|black market]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] in order to procure a cargo of spin-sealed [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna gas]], or [[Blaster gas/Legends|blaster gas]], which the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] desperately needed to supply its ships. After doing so, Natejeka was required to deliver the shipment via the ''Treasure Trove'' to the Alliance Fleet at the Rebels' predetermined [[Haven (rendezvous point)|rendezvous point]] beyond the [[Galactic Rim/Legends|Galactic Rim]] within five [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. Rieekan provided the ''Treasure Trove'' with a landing permit for Cloud City.<ref name="Freedom No More" />

The ''Treasure Trove'' never made it out of the Hoth [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The absence of the transport, which had missed its rendezvous mark by thirty-six [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]], greatly troubled Rieekan and [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]], both of whom anxiously awaited the expected delivery of the valuable [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] gas. However, the ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} starfighter escort did safely arrive at the Fleet, reporting that the transport may have been lost among the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]]. With the Fleet's supply of blaster gas running perilously low, Rieekan dispatched a team of special agents in the [[YL-2200 light freighter]] ''[[Out Runner]]'' to find and rescue the crew of the missing ''Treasure Trove''.<ref name="Freedom No More" />

===Missing among the asteroids===
Following the transport's [[Transponder code/Legends|transponder code]], the Rebel agents eventually located the crippled ''Treasure Trove'' lining the small crater of a giant [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] on the edge of the Hoth [[Asteroid field/Legends|belt]]. The transport's central command pod and upper hull remained intact, but the ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} ventral cargo pods had been completely blown away, and its [[Engine/Legends|drive system]] was blackened and badly damaged. A quick [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] scan revealed to the agents a single lifeform in the command pod.<ref name="Freedom No More" />

[[File:Chreeto.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Chreeto chased Rebel agents away from the crash site of the ''Treasure Trove''.]]
The agents boarded the ''Treasure Trove'' and found the command pod dimly lit via emergency power with only faint [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] remaining. A [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] leak had frozen what little air remained, leaving condensed water vapor on bulkhead frames. The agents soon discovered Natejeka, the lone survivor, lying in a damaged and still-docked escape pod, [[Death/Legends|dying]] from grievous injuries and exposure, and holding a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. She died immediately thereafter, but the agents learned from the datapad's contents of the ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} mission to acquire the badly needed blaster gas.<ref name="Freedom No More" />

The ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} wreck also attracted the Imperial-affiliated [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Chreeto]] the Defiant, who chased the Rebel agents away from the crash site, failing to capture them. Although the ''Treasure Trove'' went down<ref name="Freedom No More" /> as one of seventeen Rebel GR-75 medium transports destroyed during the Battle of Hoth,<ref name="Essential Atlas" /> the Rebel agents successfully completed the transport's mission by acquiring the blaster gas from Cloud City and delivering the shipment to the Alliance Fleet.<ref name="Freedom No More" />

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Treasure Trove''{{'s}} commanding [[Officer/Legends|officer]] during the Battle of Hoth was the [[Human/Legends|Human]] female Captain Amm Natejeka. Natejeka failed General Carlist Rieekan's directive to deliver a shipment of blaster gas to the Alliance Fleet, dying from her wounds and exposure to [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in the transport's command pod after the ''Treasure Trove'' was all but destroyed during its unsuccessful evacuation from Hoth.<ref name="Freedom No More" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Treasure Trove'' makes its only [[canon]]ical appearance in the [[1996]] second edition of the [[West End Games]] sourcebook [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'']] as part of a special [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] supplement entitled ''[[Freedom No More]]'', written by author [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. The ''Treasure Trove'' sets the stage for the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure, in which the players must complete the transport's mission by visiting the Hoth asteroid field and Cloud City during the [[Time/Legends|time]] frame paralleling the events in the film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="Freedom No More" />

==Appearances==
